entry.title: 🇧🇷 SAP Build: Create Event-Based Processes (São Paulo, Brazil)
entry.content:
This SAP Build low-code/no-code SAP CodeJam focuses on giving you hands-on experience for building event-triggered processes with SAP Build Process Automation, and how to work with SAP Integration Suite, advanced event mesh to send those events to your processes.

You will build an event-based scenario that lets users create S/4HANA business partners, setting off a process where:

  1. An employee approves the creation of a site access badge.
  2. A second employee, determined based on the details of the business partner, may also need to approve the badge.
  3. The process calls an external, 3rd-party badge system and waits to be called back.
  4. A user creates the badge and the badge system calls back the process instance.
  5. A notification with the badge ID is sent.

The scenario is built in 2 halves:

  • SAP Integration Suite, advanced event mesh: You will get to know all about events, CloudEvents, events in the SAP landscape, the process for publishing and subscribing to events, and your way around SAP Integration Suite, advanced event mesh. Then you will create a REST Delivery Point in advanced event mesh to forward events to your SAP Build on your SAP BTP trial account.
  • SAP Build Process Automation: You will create a process from scratch to handle the issuance of site access badges. You will learn the basics of SAP Build Process Automation (projects, triggers, conditions, creating instances, monitoring, and the Inbox), and also use some of the more recent or advanced features (event triggers, form events, waiting for a API callback).

Prerequisites

  • Bring your own laptop with power adapter and have a supported Web browser such as Google Chrome or Firefox installed on your laptop.
  • It can be helpful to bring a tablet or second laptop, if you have.
  • Complete these prerequisites BEFORE you come: 
    • Introduction to the CodeJam
    • Prerequisites

Location and Date 

This is an in-person event only (not virtual) and is planned for Wednesday, March 4, 2026 from 09:00 - 14:00 local time in São Paulo, Brazil (CET). The language of the event and the content will be English with some support in Portuguese.

Event Address: Exed Consulting (SAP AppHaus) R. James Joule, 65, 5º andar, Cidade Monções, São Paulo - SP, 04576-080

  • The event is kindly being hosted by Exed Consulting – THANK YOU!
  • The instructors are SAP Developer Advocates @KevinR and @ajmaradiaga .

Please note that spaces are limited! We can only accommodate 30 attendees. If you want to attend, register by clicking the Yes button in the Will you be attending? section above to the right. About 2 weeks before the event, you will be asked to confirm your attendance.

entry.title: 🇧🇷 Getting started with Generative AI Hub on SAP AI Core (Rio de Janeiro, Brazil)
entry.content:
This SAP CodeJam event is on Getting Started with Generative AI Hub on SAP AI Core and Python. You do not need any background in the topic, but you should have a lot of curiosity!

In this CodeJam, you will learn how to use the Generative AI Hub on SAP AI Core to implement retrieval-augmented generation (RAG). This approach improves the responses of large language models (LLMs) and reduces hallucinations. You will learn how to deploy an LLM and the orchestration service on SAP AI Core, and how to query it via SAP AI Launchpad and the Python SDK. Additionally, you will gain insights into key generative AI concepts and learn how to enhance RAG responses.

This is a free in-person event and is planned for Thursday, March 5, 2026 in Rio de Janeiro from 09:00 to 14:00. The language of the event and the content will be in English with some support in Portuguese.

The event is kindly hosted by INFNET Institute. Thank you!

The instructors are Developer Advocates @KevinR and @ajmaradiaga  

Location: INFNET Institute - Rua São José - Centro, Rio de Janeiro - State of Rio de Janeiro, Brazil

Please note that spaces are limited; we can only accommodate 30 attendees. If you wish to attend, register by clicking the Yes button in the Will you be attending? section above to the right. A few weeks before the event, we will ask you to confirm your attendance.

Prerequisites: Any Chromium-based web browser, such as Microsoft Edge, Google Chrome etc.

Visit the SAP CodeJam group for more information on SAP CodeJam events, to ask questions, and to chat with your fellow attendees.

entry.title: Re: Electronic Account Statement
entry.content:
Hi @bahattin_orbis ,

I'm afraid I can't help you completely - however, I'd recommend you to have a look at the following help content if you're using the product SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition. You might find the info you're looking for there: 

  • Interpretation Algorithms
  • Overview on All Predelivered Processing Instructions
  • Configuration for Bank Statement Processing

Furthermore, I'd recommend you to start using processing rules to process your bank statements. You can create and define your processing rules in the Manage Processing Rules app and select certain processing instructions. For more information, see the following help content and the related links at the end of each page:

  • Manage Processing Rules - For Bank Statements
  • How to Create Processing Rules

I hope this helps you furhter. 

Best regards,
Anita

entry.title: How to configure managed approuter to serve CAP endpoints?
entry.content:
Hi all,

I have a fullstack CAP cloud application along with a managed approuter. I am having some trouble getting the approuter to correctly point to my cap service (currently without a UI). Currently the approuter correctly points to my ui5 page (capbase) but my cap service (dev-capbase-srv) returns the error message 

Cannot GET / 

However, when I append the /odata/v4/catalog/Employees to my url I get a 401 unauthorized error. But I have tested this via postman by generating a bearer token and can then see the data displayed. But my question is why is my approuter not directing me to this service automatically without having to append the /odata/v4/catalog/Employee endpoint to my url: port4004-workspaces-ws-ak3uq.us10.trial.applicationstudio.cloud.sap/

Further information: I have a fiori list report generated in my app folder for this CAP employee service
my xs-app.json file is as follows:

{
  "routes": [
    {
      "source": "^/odata/v4/catalog/(.*)$",
      "target": "/odata/v4/catalog/$1",
      "destination": "srv-api",
      "csrfProtection": false,
      "authenticationType": "xsuaa"
    }
  ],
  "welcomeFile": "/interfacebase"
}

mta.yaml 

_schema-version: 3.3.0
ID: capbase
description: A simple CAP project.
version: 1.0.0
modules:
- name: capbase-srv
  type: nodejs
  path: gen/srv
  requires:
  - name: capbase-auth
  provides:
  - name: srv-api
    properties:
      srv-url: ${default-url}
  parameters:
    buildpack: nodejs_buildpack
    instances: 1
    memory: 256M
  build-parameters:
    builder: npm-ci
- name: capbase
  type: approuter.nodejs
  path: app/router
  requires:
  - name: srv-api
    group: destinations
    properties:
      forwardAuthToken: true
      name: srv-api
      url: ~{srv-url}
  - name: capbase-auth
  - name: capbase-html5-runtime
  - name: capbase-destination-service
    group: destinations
    properties:
      forwardAuthToken: false
      name: ui5
      url: https://ui5.sap.com
  provides:
  - name: app-api
    properties:
      app-protocol: ${protocol}
      app-uri: ${default-uri}
  parameters:
    disk-quota: 256M
    keep-existing-routes: true
    memory: 256M
- name: capbase-app-deployer
  type: com.sap.application.content
  path: gen
  requires:
  - name: capbase-auth
  - name: srv-api
  - name: capbase-html5-repo-host
    parameters:
      config:
        HTML5Runtime_enabled: true
      content-target: true
  build-parameters:
    build-result: app/
    requires:
    - artifacts:
      - interfacebase.zip
      name: interfacebase
      target-path: app/
    - artifacts:
      - employeeui.zip
      name: employeeui
      target-path: app/
- name: interfacebase
  type: html5
  path: app/interfacebase
  build-parameters:
    build-result: dist
    builder: custom
    commands:
    - npm install
    - npm run build:cf
    supported-platforms: []
- name: employeeui
  type: html5
  path: app/employeeui
  build-parameters:
    build-result: dist
    builder: custom
    commands:
    - npm install
    - npm run build:cf
    supported-platforms: []
resources:
- name: capbase-auth
  type: org.cloudfoundry.managed-service
  parameters:
    config:
      oauth2-configuration:
        credential-types:
        - binding-secret
        - x509
        redirect-uris:
        - https://*~{app-api/app-uri}/**
      tenant-mode: dedicated
      xsappname: capbase-${org}-${space}
    path: ./xs-security.json
    service: xsuaa
    service-plan: application
  requires:
  - name: app-api
- name: capbase-html5-repo-host
  type: org.cloudfoundry.managed-service
  parameters:
    service: html5-apps-repo
    service-plan: app-host
- name: capbase-html5-runtime
  type: org.cloudfoundry.managed-service
  parameters:
    service: html5-apps-repo
    service-plan: app-runtime
- name: capbase-destination-service
  type: org.cloudfoundry.managed-service
  parameters:
    config:
      HTML5Runtime_enabled: false
      init_data:
        instance:
          destinations:
          - Authentication: NoAuthentication
            Name: ui5
            ProxyType: Internet
            Type: HTTP
            URL: https://ui5.sap.com
          existing_destinations_policy: update
      version: 1.0.0
    service: destination
    service-name: capbase-destination-service
    service-plan: lite
parameters:
  deploy_mode: html5-repo
  enable-parallel-deployments: true
build-parameters:
  before-all:
  - builder: custom
    commands:
    - npm ci
    - npx cds build --production

entry.title: WebI - Calculate the sum of daily average values across a specifc date range
entry.content:
Hello WebI Gurus,

I need to report some SAP Data where for a single date several time values / time bookings exist (the first "Stunden" column in the table below). This data should be summed per date.

The second time value column is a join from a worktime per day table -> only one value for each date exists -> same value for every time booking record is joined. (second "Stunden" column in the table below).
This value I need in the report is  average per date.

In my Webi Report I got a summarized table (aggregating the data across all dates) on the left and a table showing the data per date on the right.

Right table is working correctly - strangely projection average seems to be applied although I switched in IDT business layer to from average to none.

Left table displays same data even if date is not in drill down.
When I use =Sum([Soll Stunden])  I get #MULTIVALUE error.
I tried function =Average([Soll Stunden]) In ([Datum])  as variable but this delivers the average across all dates and not the average per day summed up across all dates.

 

Would really appreciate any help in regards how to solve this issue.

Thanks and best regards
Axel

Is there any way to replicate EDQA in AATP without having to run BOP every x minutes? I have looked at the roadmap but I haven't seen any mention for this yet.

Thanks a lot already!

entry.title: Re: SAP Integration Architecture Guide
entry.content:
At the risk of stating the obvious but when it comes to ETL (a moniker not found on this page) SAP offers this

  • SDI (Roadmap only entries of supporting different versions of connector systems like Db2 etc)
  • SLT (Roadmap last entry 2023)
  • Edge Integration Cell (not really a ETL but a process driven local integration runtime of Cloud Integration / Integration Suite - Costing an arm and a leg for mass data replication with 0.5 x messages of regular Cloud Integration)

That's it folks, or did I miss something essential?

entry.title: Re: Production Integration with Warehouse Management in S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition
entry.content:
@Christian_Ries excellent compilation of options for mapping production material requirements.
Thank you very much!

However, I have I very specific question regarding the creation of warehouse orders and warehouse tasks for production material requests.

I have a scenario related to “Extended Production Integration - 6NI” with the following settings:
Single order staging and calculation based on PMRs

If I now create two production orders, e.g., 500000 (PRM = 910000000000) and 500001 (PRM = 910000000001) and then use the “Create Warehouse Tasks - Staging for Production” app to create the warehouse tasks, the system bundles the warehouse tasks for different PMRs into one warehouse order.

However, in my case, this is explicitly not what I want. I would like to receive a single warehouse order per PMR with several warehouse tasks of the same PMR.

I want to know A) why the system bundles several PMRs into one warehouse order in single order staging and B) what I need to set or configure in the system to define the PMR as a split criterion for warehouse order creation?

Thank you in advance!

entry.title: Willkommen!
entry.content:
Herzlich willkommen an alle Interessierten aus dem Raum Basel (CH), aus der Region Freiburg (DE) und natürlich auch aus dem schönen Elsass!

Wir möchten einen offenen, ungezwungenen SAP-Stammtisch aufbauen und laden Entwickler, Berater und Nutzer ein, sich regelmässig mit uns zu treffen – abwechselnd in Basel und in Freiburg. Im Mittelpunkt steht der lockere Austausch rund um SAP: miteinander ins Gespräch kommen, Fragen stellen, Erfahrungen teilen, voneinander lernen und sich vernetzen.

Ob ihr etwas essen möchtet oder einfach nur auf ein Getränk vorbeikommt, bleibt ganz euch überlassen. Wichtig ist uns eine entspannte Atmosphäre, in der sich jede und jeder wohlfühlt. Kommt vorbei, bringt gerne weitere Interessierte mit und gestaltet diesen SAP-Stammtisch gemeinsam mit uns!

Alle, die die Idee des offenen Austauschs und der Zusammenarbeit teilen, sind eingeladen, sich einzubringen, Themen vorzuschlagen oder zukünftige Events mitzugestalten.

Grüsse Marcel und Bastian

entry.title: Entry opportunities into SAP for experienced leaders from technical backgrounds
entry.content:
Hi everyone,

I have led teams of up to 100 people in technical and craft-based environments and have strong experience in operations, processes and day-to-day business workflows.

Any guidance or experience would be appreciated.

 

Thanks!

entry.title: Re: Trigger Campaign Based on Customer Specific Link Clic...

Hi colleague,
Yes, this can be done in Emarsys.
Using the Email Behavior Check node in Automation Center, you can continue the journey when a contact clicks a specific link in a previous email.

https://help.sap.com/docs/SAP_EMARSYS/f8e2fafeea804018a954a8857d9dfff3/20cd14b8e4184d77ab7eccf04234c056.html?locale=en-US&state=PRODUCTION&version=CLOUD

Best regards.
Fernando DJ Espitia.

entry.title: Re: Use SAP SuccessFactors Compensation to Provide Pay Equity Adjustments
entry.content: In this setup, something is not quite right, because the average is calculated based on current salaries + merits, while the Average Male FTE Salary column is static. This column is not updated when merits are adjusted throughout the compensation process. As a result, an inconsistency arises: individual salaries change, but the average remains the same, even though it should change accordingly — or perhaps I am overlooking something?

entry.title: SAP Task Center cannot connect SAP BPA because Identity_Authentication_Connectivity_IDS not recogniz
entry.content:
Hey there,

we got a strange situation. We have set up SAP Work Zone, advanced edition in one subaccount and also SAP Task Center in this subaccount. It works fine. Even tasks of Work Zone get into the Task Center. We have a destination for Identity_Authentication_Connectivity_IDS in this subaccount, and the service status is also okay.

So now we want to add SAP Build Process Automation with plan build-default that lives in another subaccount. So we got into the subaccount of Work Zone and Task Center and created the destination SAPBuildPA_rem, as it is in another subaccount. As soon as we enable the Task Center on this destination with tc.enabled=true, Task Center Administration will recognize it, but say there is an error. I think it was something like Identity_Authentication_Connectivity_IDS also needs to be there, and somehow the service tab then also showed errors for the service now. We added the Identity_Authentication_Connectivity_IDS destination as well for the subaccount of SAP BPA, even though it's not stated in the documentation. But somehow it will still fail for the SAPBuildPA_rem destination in the Task Center Administration and say Identity_Authentication_Connectivity_IDS could not be found for subaccount ID <where SAP BPA lives>, which is not true, as this destination exists.

What is wrong?

Due to connectivity issues the next try to get task data will start in 30 seconds. Exception: {"error":{"code":"sap.workflowservice.ids.unavailable","message":"Unable to find destination Identity_Authentication_Connectivity_IDS for tenant: '********************'"}}

 

We have problems in general that we can also not get SAP Build Process Automation into Work Zone advanced. The inbox also states an error. The authentication of SAP BPA is behind a corporate IDP, and for Work Zone and Task Center, it is not. But this still makes no sense for the main Task Center error.

Kind regards

entry.title: Re: Is SAP still investing and supporting B1if going forw...
entry.content:
Hello,

Thank you for your question.

SAP Business One Integration Framework remains an important component and tool for SAP Business One product and partners & customers. It has several key benefits in integration efforts, such as:

  • Simple to install and runs on SAP HANA and Microsoft SQL Server
  • Can integrate SAP and non-SAP  systems,  web and cloud-based applications, and services
  • Including message and error handling, business process control, tracking, logging, transaction control, security
  • Uses established integration standards such as XML and XSLT

We are further investing into the solution and ensuring the tool's high security and quality.  Please see the slide from official SAP Business One product roadmap related to the planned Integration Framework innovations:

https://www.sap.com/products/erp/business-one/features.html?pdf-asset=484c875d-c37c-0010-82c7-eda71af511fa&page=1

Peter DOMINIK

Global Head of SAP Business One Product Management

entry.title: Characteristic values defined by dependencies not stored in table V_IBIN_SYVAL
entry.content:
Hi all,

I have created a query that does the following:

  1. Based on the sales order and the sales order item, find CUOBJ in table VBAP.
  2. Link CUOBJ to field INSTANCE in table IBIN and retrieve IN_RECNO
  3. Link IN_RECNO with IN_RECNO in table V_IBIN_SYVAL to retrieve the charteristic values (field ATWRT). 

At point 3 is where the issues lies. For most characteristics, the value is not found in table V_IBIN_SYVAL. All these values come from dependency or are a manual antry, e.g. entering the outside diameter.

Why are these characteristic values not displayed in table V_IBIN_SYVAL? 

Are there other tables that I can use to retrieve the values entered for the specific configuration? FM VC_I_GET_CONFIGURATION is not an option.

entry.title: How to display total values on top of each bar in a 100% stacked column chart in SAC
entry.content:
Hi everyone,
I’m using a 100% stacked column chart in SAP Analytics Cloud (Story). Each column currently shows percentage labels per segment.
I would like to also display the absolute total per column—ideally on top of the column, without losing the percentage labels.

Questions:

  1. Is there a built-in option in SAC to display both percentage per segment and the absolute total per column at the same time in a 100% stacked chart?
  2. If not, what best practices / workarounds do you recommend?

 

Thanks and best regards

https://<production subdomain>.eu10.sapdas.cloud.sap/resources/public/webclient/s4

entry.title: Re: How to group Business Roles and Role Upgrades in SAP...
entry.content:
Dear @duynd23,

Question 1.

No, Public Cloud does not support combining multiple business roles into a composite role the way you might know it from classic role concepts

SAP Note below:

https://me.sap.com/notes/0003635967

What you can do instead in Public Cloud:

  • Assign multiple business roles directly to the same user a business user can have one or more business roles

More details below:

https://learning.sap.com/courses/implementing-sap-s-4hana-cloud-public-edition/creating-and-customizing-business-roles

https://learning.sap.com/courses/exploring-the-fundamentals-of-sap-system-security/maintaining-business-roles-in-sap-s-4hana-public-cloud

  • Create a single “bundle” business role that contains all required business catalogs from the roles you wanted to group. This is explicitly the workaround SAP points to.
  • Use Business Role Groups only for organization/search/delegation, not for provisioning access. You cannot assign a role group to a user to grant authorizations

SAP Note below:

https://me.sap.com/notes/0003354117

Practical approach that recommended:

  1. Open Maintain Business Roles
  2. Create a new business role or copy from a template role
  3. Add the needed Business Catalogs from the multiple roles you wanted to group
  4. Maintain Restrictions as required
  5. Assign spaces/pages if relevant
  6. Assign this single role to users in Maintain Business Users

Question 2.

No, the Public Cloud authorization model is based on assigning Business Roles to Business Users, there’s no reference user that you assign to other users concept for reusing role sets

Best Public Cloud alternatives:

  • Use the bundle role described above (single assignment, consistent access package)
  • Use Leading Business Role + derived roles

More details below:

https://learning.sap.com/learning-journeys/performing-advanced-configuration-and-role-management-in-sap-s4hana-cloud-public-edition-ehs-environment-management/managing-business-role-changes-after-upgrade_e68f44fc-6a4c-4b0c-8b34-f7c8c65f5368

https://learning.sap.com/courses/exploring-the-fundamentals-of-sap-system-security/maintaining-business-roles-in-sap-s-4hana-public-cloud

  • If your goal is operational efficiency (many users): maintain roles once, then assign the role to multiple users via Maintain Business Users

More details below:

https://learning.sap.com/courses/implementing-sap-s-4hana-cloud-public-edition/creating-and-customizing-business-roles

Question 3.

In Public Cloud, copied/custom business roles do not automatically inherit template updates. After an upgrade, you should use SAP post-upgrade tools to review deltas and then apply template changes to your roles where appropriate

  • Template deltas:
    1. Open Manage Business Role Changes After Upgrade
    2. In Business Role Templates area, select the template showing changes
    3. Select impacted business roles and choose Compare with Business Role Template
    4. In the comparison, either Apply All or select specific deviations to apply
    5. Optionally define one role as leading so changes propagate to the selected roles
    6. Save
  • Restriction type changes:
    • The app shows changed restriction types so you can mass-maintain restrictions for impacted roles where access categories match

More details below:

https://fioriappslibrary.hana.ondemand.com/sap/fix/externalViewer/#/detail/Apps('F6955')/S36

Good practice for Public Cloud. Do this first in your test/quality system after the upgrade, then move the adjusted roles according to your change process for example via software collections in a 3-system landscape

Best Regards,

Dawid

entry.title: Re: S/4HANA Public Cloud – Extensibility for Supplier Reb...
entry.content:
Good day @zeck9762 

Thank you for your question.

Due to the clean core public cloud limitation, you cannot extend the catalog of the delivered Business Volume Selection Criteria field with an in app custom field or from your product master and expect it to appear as selectable in your condition contract.

The public cloud however, offers you the possibilities to:

  • Add custom fields to condition contract and settlement document e.g., for display, to enable you copy or validations when you use app extensibility. See Custom Fields in Settlement Documents for more details.
  • Configure and use standard business volume selection criteria that is provided for condition contracts and manage snapshots and critical change through customising.

So, the answer to your questions about the following are provided below:

1. In Public Cloud, is it possible to extend Business Volume Selection Criteria (add new fields or new field combinations) for supplier rebates / condition contracts?

Answer: No, it is not possible in public cloud.

2. If the answer is “not directly”, is there any supported way to implement custom logic to read Product Master custom fields during business volume determination?

Answer: Typically, not for public cloud selection criteria itself. Unless SAP explicitly releases a specific key use user BAdI, which is uncommon.

3. If there is no supported extension for this, what is the recommended standard approach (mapping the attribute to an available standard field like product hierarchy/material group, or restructuring master data/pricing logic) while staying within clean-core principles?

Answer: I would recommend mapping the attribute into your product Hierarchy or another standard characteristic you can select, then use it in Business Volume Selection. That is a clean core approach and it keeps it simple.

You can reference: Custom Fields in Settlement Documents and Condition Contract for more details.

Let me know if you have further questions.

Best regards

Chris

entry.title: SAP Certification update: Practical exams roll out in 2026
entry.content:
Last November, we announced one of the most significant shifts in SAP Certification history: a move from traditional, multiple-choice exams to performance-based, practical, open-book assessments that reflect how SAP professionals actually work. The response from the SAP Community has been extraordinary.

More than 1,300 SAP TechEd attendees registered for onsite exams across Berlin, Bangalore, and Louisville, taking advantage of the opportunity to try the new model with authentic SAP systems and AI-supported scenarios. “I took the practical exam for the SAP Build Developer certification at SAP TechEd in Berlin and was impressed with how it reflected actual, on-the-job problem solving,” said Antonieta Lopez, an SAP Champion. “I needed some help in one task and was able to quickly solve the problem using AI, just as I would in real life. I really like the new certification exams because they are like real business instead of learning things by heart.”

Thousands more have embraced the new exam model, achieving practical certifications in Generative AI Developers, SAP Build Developers, SAP Business Data Cloud,  Solution Architects for SAP Business Technology Platform, SAP BTP System Administrators and RISE with SAP Methodology and Experience.

In the weeks following SAP TechEd, we’ve been busy transitioning all certification exams to the practical model and have been very pleased with the response from our community. Here are a few new practical exams you might want to explore first:

  • Positioning SAP Business AI Solutions as part of SAP Business Suite
  • Positioning SAP Business Suite
  • RISE with SAP Methodology and Experience
  • SAP Business Data Cloud
  • SAP Generative AI Developer
  • SAP SuccessFactors Employee Central Core and Position Management

Browse all practical exams  

Certification for a world where AI is part of the workday

If you’ve prepared for certification on the SAP Learning site, you’ve already experienced practical exercises and scenario-based tasks as part of the learning journeys. The new exams simply extend that learning approach, so the moment you begin your certification, it already feels familiar.

Every practical exam, whether system-based or scenario-based, mirrors the reality of SAP projects:

  • Timeboxed tasks in SAP systems
  • Realistic customer or project scenarios
  • AI-supported simulations
  • Open book: Access to resources like SAP Help Portal, SAP Learning site, SAP Community, and SAP Joule for Consultants (where applicable).
  • A clear focus on proving your ability to do the work

This shift is about trust: for learners who invest in their careers, and for employers who rely on SAP-certified professionals to deliver results from day one.

Be an early leader in the next era of SAP skills

As we roll out the full portfolio of performance-based certification exams in 2026, we want to encourage the community to take part. Not because certification exams will be easier (they won’t be) but because those who demonstrate practical skills now will help define what excellence looks like going forward.

We’re building a more trusted, meaningful, and future-ready certification framework and we invite you to be part of the shaping its future. To explore the new practical exams, visit the SAP Certification reimagined page and read the FAQ.

entry.title: Technical RFC: Building an "Auditable Autopilot" for Material Shortages – Where is the real Excel He
entry.content:
Hi everyone,
I’m an AI developer in Germany architecting a "Human-in-the-loop" agent to automate exception handling for discrete manufacturers (Mittelstand).
The goal is to reduce the 4+ hours of manual coordination that happens when a supplier is late or a carrier delay occurs, leading to potential line stops. We are focusing on a safe, auditable "exception autopilot" model rather than a generic "digital brain."
I have a few "reality check" questions for the consultants and experts here. I need brutal honesty so I don't build a product that fails in a real German factory environment:
  1. The 'Shadow' System: When a delay hits, does the rescheduling happen in SAP (e.g., MD04/CO06) immediately, or do planners usually run a 'Shadow Excel' for 1-2 days first to figure out the impact?
  2. The Data Gap: What’s the one piece of information that is never in SAP (e.g., real-time carrier GPS, unstructured email updates) that forces a human to manually 'bridge' the gap?
  3. The 'Green Button' Barrier: If an AI drafted the SAP update and the supplier email, what is the #1 reason a German Plant Manager would not press 'Approve'? (e.g., legal liability, lack of audit trail, trust issues).
  4. On-Premise vs. Cloud: For a mid-sized manufacturer, would the IT department demand this system runs 100% on-premise for data sovereignty, or is a highly controlled cloud connection (like SAP BTP) acceptable now in 2026?
Looking forward to the insights from the community so we can build something that actually solves the real pain points.
Thanks in advance for your expertise!

entry.title: Re: Custom Document Types & Custom Asset Classes in Publi...
entry.content:
Good day @muhammad_yousufali 

Thank you for your question.

Provided you carry out your configuration through the CBC and SSCUI tools, you do not need to worry about your configuration after any SAP system upgrade. The public cloud ensures your configurations remains available after upgrades. However, you should still carry out regression test after each upgrade because SAP can deliver new functions or new mandatory checks. See Upgrade Guidance for some insights.

Blog: Review Business Role Changes before a Major Upgrade in the SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition also provides essential information.

Regarding your question you creating your own “Z” type documents, in many areas this is possible if the SSCUI exists for that scope. The public cloud generally exposes document type configuration through specific SSCUIs. That means generally:

  • You can copy a delivered document type and adjust only the fields that is allowed in that SSCUI.
  • Some fields can be read only or greyed out in public cloud.

So basically, you can create or adjust your document types where a configuration activity is provided. See KBA 3233543 and KBA 3474851 for more details.

On your question about creating “Z” Asset Classes, you can create custom asset classes but not with the “Z prefix” because Ids are numeric codes. Also creating customer asset classes through “Define Asset Classes”.

I recommend you:

  • Use a custom number range for asset class IDs rather than a “Z” prefix.
  • Make use of “Z” naming in your description if you want clear customer identification.

To summarise:

  • Yes, to creating asset classes but use numeric customer range.
  • Custom document types, only if your scope provides the SSCUI and it allows it.
  • Test your configurations after every upgrade.

 

Best regards

Chris

entry.title: Re: SAP MDK – Responsive layout support
entry.content:
Yes, the MDK pages use responsive controls that will layout based on the size of the device.   For example on a phone a control may render the caption on one line and the value on the second line in portrait but if using landscape render caption and value on the same line.  Another example is some sections support multiple columns when running on a tablet but not on a phone.

For the Section Button Table there is a Layout Type option that can be set to horizontal to get side by side buttons depending on the length of the button Title.  So depending on the number of buttons and the length of the Titles it will render side by side based on the space available before wrapping beneath the first buttons.

entry.title: Event Enabling Idocs rapidly and almost effortless via BTP Integration Suite, Cloud Integration
entry.content:
1) Introduction

In the past we have published a couple of Blogs where we describe how to event enable Idocs via Event Enablement AddOn or Application Interface Framework. Another possibility with the least configuration on the ERP (ECC or S/4) System is the usage of the HTTP Port and SAP Cloud Integration. This Blog will demonstrate how to easily accomplish this + how to dynamically add Topic Values with the Help of the Partner Directory in SAP Cloud Integration.  

 

2) ERP Customizing

Let´s start with the Definition of the Logical System in Transaction SALE

I have defined this Logical System for the Connection to AEM via Cloud Integration

In SM59 I have already prepared a Destination to SAP Cloud Integration Iflow receiving the Idocs:

Of course later we´ll have to make sure that the Iflow Address and the endpoint in this Destination are the same. Based on this Destination we create a Port in WE21:

 

Then we´ll need a Partner Profile in WE20 with the Receiver Port we just created. Let´s say we want to distribute Vendor Master Data with CREMAS:

Also we´ll need a Distribution Model in BD64:

(Only the Receiver CPI_AEM relevant here)

That is all we have to do on the ERP! Let´s switch to the Cloud Integration.

 

2) SAP Cloud Integration

Static Topic

In my Github Repo I have provided a Template Iflow you can upload into your Cloud Integration Package 

First thing is to configure the Sender Address and Role:

In the Content Modifier Step we retrieve the Idoc Headers from the Payload:

Then, depending if the Idoc has a CIMTYP or not, we put together the Topic. 

With CIMTYP:

Header "Topic" = "${header.SAP_IDoc_EDIDC_SNDPRN}/${header.SAP_IDoc_EDIDC_MESTYP}/${header.SAP_IDoc_EDIDC_IDOCTYP}/${header.SAP_IDoc_EDIDC_CIMTYP}"

Without CIMTYP the Topic is filled like this: "${header.SAP_IDoc_EDIDC_SNDPRN}/${header.SAP_IDoc_EDIDC_MESTYP}/${header.SAP_IDoc_EDIDC_IDOCTYP}"

So if I have an CREMAS07 Idoc from my System T33, the Topic would be 

T33CLNT800/CREMAS/CREMAS07

If this is already enough for you, you can ignore or even remove the 2 Groovy Scripts after the Router in the Iflow.

The only Thing left of course is the Receiver Channel. Here we have to maintain the Connection Parameters

That´s it. You can deploy the Iflow!

Dynamic Topic

If we want to include Parts of the Payload into the Topic, we´ll have to get it via Xpath. This could be easily done via Content Modifier, but to keep everything dynamic in one Iflow, I decided to implement 2 Groovy Scripts. 1st Script is retrieving the XPaths I set up for my Idoc Type in the Partner Directory. 2nd Script performs the XPath on the Body and puts together the Topic.

The Maintainence of the Idoc-specific XPaths happens in the Partner Directory. So in Monitor -> Integration and APIs -> Partner Directory

The PartnerID is always <MESTYP>.<IDOCTYP>.<CIMTYP> (if CIMTYP exists) or <MESTYP>.<IDOCTYP> (no CIMTYP). In my Case it is CREMAS.CREMAS07. I have defined these 3 XPaths

Test

Now let´s test the Scenario. I set the Log Level on the Iflow to "Trace" and set up a Consumer on Advanced Event Mesh TryMe Feature on Topic "T33CLNT800/>" 

 

Now let´s send a Vendor from the ERP System Transaction BD14:

Result:

Let´s check in WE05:

Now let´s check the Trace in SAP Cloud Integration Message Monitoring:

Here we can see at the Receiver Channel Step the dynamic Topic we produced:

Also on Advanced Event Mesh we can see the result in TryMe:

That´s it! Happy Eventing!

entry.title: Re: Move to SAP S/4HANA with Selective Data Transition
entry.content:
This is an excellent and very timely breakdown of Selective Data Transition. The clarification between Shell Conversion and Mix & Match is especially helpful — many customers still treat SDT as a single method rather than a spectrum of options.

In the utility programs I’ve been part of, SDT has been most valuable when organizations need phased go‑lives or want to preserve specific process areas while modernizing others. Your point about balancing the reduced cutover risk against the operational complexity of running two live ERP systems is spot‑on. That trade‑off is often underestimated.

The reference to the updated Activate roadmap and the Business Transformation Center is also appreciated. These newer tools are becoming increasingly relevant for customers who want a more structured, lower‑risk SDT path.

Thanks for keeping this topic current — especially with the 2025 updates. It’s a great resource for teams evaluating their path to S/4HANA.

entry.title: How to configure eSignature with DocuSign and SAP Fieldglass
entry.content:
SAP Fieldglass supports the needs of Buyers to have documents electronically signed with DocuSign as the eSignature provider.

In this blog, we’ll cover everything you need to know to configure an integration between DocuSign and SAP Fieldglass to support your eSignature workflow.

You can also download this information as a one-pager here.

Prerequisites & Required Resources

Before you get started, you must ensure that your company has at least one DocuSign account established:

  • Details from your DocuSign account are required to set up the integration between SAP Fieldglass and DocuSign
  • Contact your SAP representative if you have any questions

If your company has a DocuSign account, ensure the correct resources are engaged to support the setup of this integration:

  • A user with SAP Fieldglass Configuration Manager access is required to create the DocuSign End Point
  • A user with SAP Fieldglass Administrator access is required to create a DocuSign eSignature Template, Activity Items and Checklists, and update User permissions 
  • A DocuSign Administrator is required to provide the relevant details from your account to create a DocuSign Template


Configuration Steps

Once the prerequisites and resources are in place, follow these steps to set up the integration between DocuSign and SAP Fieldglass:

  1. The first step is to raise a case with SAP Fieldglass to enable the company configuration: eSignature Provider – DocuSign
  2. Once the company configuration has been enabled, the SAP Fieldglass Configuration Manager can create the DocuSign End Point
    • A DocuSign Administrator will need to provide the following values from your DocuSign account to configure the End Point:

      SAP Fieldglass End Point (mandatory fields)
      Required values from your DocuSign account

      End Point

      Account Base URI located under My Account Information

      Integrator Key

      Integrator Key for the SAP Fieldglass application is located under Apps and Integrator Keys

      •        Note: Before completing this step, SAP Fieldglass may need to be added by selecting Add App and Integration Key

      •        The DocuSign Administrator must add the value http://localhost to the Redirect URI field by selecting Edit > Additional Settings > Add URI

      User ID

      User ID located under My Account Information

      Private Key

      The DocuSign Administrator will need to generate the RSA key pair located under Service Integration > Generate RSA



  3. The next step is to request application consent for SAP Fieldglass to support eSignature workflows
    • Details on how to request application consent can be found on the DocuSign Developers portal > Using JSON Web Token (JWT) Grant page
    • The process involves constructing a URI with a base path for the application service (test or production), your requested scope, integration key, and redirect URI. It will look like the URI shown here:
    • https://account-d.docusign.com/oauth/auth?response_type=code&scope=signature%20impersonation&client_id=YOUR_INTEGRATION_KEY&redirect_uri=http://localhost
    • The URI needs to be copied and paste into a browser to accept and allow access

  4. Next, the DocuSign Administrator creates a DocuSign Template in DocuSign
    • DocuSign Templates allow you to standardize the signing process by predefining certain information like recipient roles, recipient emails, signature types, and signing fields

  5. The SAP Fieldglass Administrator then creates a DocuSign eSignature Provider Template in SAP Fieldglass
    • This step essentially provides the link to your company's templates in DocuSign
  6. Any users who will be allowed to electronically sign documents will need to be identified and have the User can be Selected to Electronically Sign a Document permission enabled
    • This permission can be enabled directly on the user profile page or updates can be completed in bulk using the User Upload connector

  7. Configure your company's eSignature workflow with Activity Items and (optional) Activity Checklist
    • Your desired contract workflow will determine which Activity Items need to be setup
      • For example, a Buyer may require an initial step, such as document validation, prior to triggering the DocuSign integration
    • At a minimum, an Activity Item with the Completion Type: Initiate eSignature Process must be configured to kick-off the eSignature workflow
    • Multiple workflows can be setup and used for different types of documents that require electronic signature


Helpful Resources

  • Watch the Configuration Manager: How to configure eSignature with DocuSign tutorial video for detailed, step-by-step instructions on how to set up the integration 
  • Download this one-pager for a quick overview of the prerequisites, required resources, and steps to configure eSignature with DocuSign

entry.title: Sneak Peek in to SAP Analytics Cloud release for Q1 2026
entry.content:
As we move toward the end of January we take a sneak peek in to our Top 5 features in SAP Analytics Cloud for QRC1 2026. A full list of what is coming can be found on our Roadmap Explorer.  Let's dive in!

New Chart Types

To kick off the year we are expanding the native visualization capabilities available with the introduction of new Pareto and area chart types designed to simplify advanced visualization and analysis. The Pareto chart automatically performs 80/20 analysis using built‑in running‑total logic and allows the addition of comparison measures without affecting the core calculation. The new area chart enhances trend exploration through more intuitive visual breakdowns.

Together, these new chart types reduce reliance on custom widgets, streamline dashboard creation, and mark the beginning of a broader roadmap to deliver more out‑of‑the‑box visualizations that elevate analytical flexibility and user experience.

 

Story Versioning

Staying with Story enhancements this quarter we introduce a new story versioning framework enables designers to create, manage, and restore up to ten major versions of a story, providing greater control over iterative development. Story Designers can access version history, open previous versions in a read-only format, restore them, or save them as new artifacts, supporting safe experimentation without risking published content.

This capability ensures transparency in change tracking and simplifies collaboration across teams.

As the foundational phase of a larger multi artifact versioning roadmap, it establishes the technical basis for extending version control to additional SAP Analytics Cloud asset types in future releases.

 

 

Comment Management

We know how important collaboration is for our customers, so this quarter we have introduced a new comment management dashboard that enables a centralized and streamlined way to to manage comments across SAP Analytics Cloud models and versions. This gives Administrators visibility into comment statistics, including threads, data‑point comments, and dimension comment, enabling more effective governance and auditability.

Bulk actions, such as deleting comments at a version or model level and copying comments across versions within Model, simplify administrative workflows and help maintain clean, compliant environments.

 

 

My Metrics for KPIs monitoring: Alerting 

Continuing to enhance our "My Metrics" framework introduced introduced at the end of 2025 with lots of new features for an even more personalized experience. These include:

  • Subscribe or unsubscribe to metrics reports for continuous change tracking
  • Set report frequency by day, week, or month
  • Share reports with other users or teams 
  • Receive reports by e-mail or through the notification center 

 

 

Live data connectivity to Snowflake

In this release we are excited to announce that SAP Analytics Cloud has extended its external live data access for planning capable models to Snowflake.

This connection reduces data loading overhead, simplifies model configuration, and lowers total cost of ownership by allowing customers to work directly with Snowflake data as a live external version. Once the data access agent is enabled, users can create planning capable models based on Snowflake sources, benefiting from the same performance considerations and modeling flexibility available for existing live connections such as Google BigQuery.

 

There are plenty more exciting deliverables coming your way this quarter and we will be taking a deeper dive into these items in our What’s New video and blog series so stay tuned. For a full list of all features coming in QRC1 2026 for SAP Analytics Cloud check out the Roadmap Explorer  and the What's New Viewer

Want to experience new and existing features for yourself? Take the leap and start your journey towards making data-driven decisions with confidence by signing up for a free trial, today.

entry.title: Error While Filtering Goods Receipts by "DocTime"
entry.content:
Hello,

   I have a problem and I was wondering if anyone may have a suggestion.

Problem: I am trying to filter goods receipt forms that were created after a particular date and time. I'm using SAP's Service Layer REST API.

 Here is my GET request:

GET InventoryGenEntries?$filter=DocDate ge '2025-12-12T14:00:00Z' and DocTime ge '2025-12-12T14:00:00Z'

The expected response should be a nice json stream with a collection of Goods Receipt forms. Instead, I get the following json:

{
    "error" : {
        "code" : 299,
        "message" : {
            "lang" : "en-us",
            "value" : "Internal server error."
        }
    }
}

I already tried all the possible date and time formats documented in the SAP official Service Layer guide. Best case scenario I get a "Query string error - the given value('90000') of property 'DocTime' is of invalid time format", worst case scenario I get an "Internal server error."

And then, I found this KB: 2596415 - Error While Filtering Marketing Documents by "DocTime"

https://me.sap.com/notes/2596415/E

That KB is from 8 years ago, and a previous version of SAP. Plenty of patches have been published ever since.

Here is my environment: SAP Business One 10 sp 2505, SQL 2016 - fresh from three months ago.

Any help is greatly appreciated!

entry.title: Train-the-Trainers: SAP S/4HANA Global Bike 4.3
entry.content:
Join our exclusive 4-day Train-the-Trainers workshop designed especially for SAP educators and professors who want to deepen their knowledge of SAP S/4HANA and enhance the way they teach enterprise process management.

This hands-on training will be led by Marcela Revilla, Senior SAP Consultant Instructor, a highly experienced professional with multiple SAP certifications and extensive expertise in SAP education and implementation projects.

Throughout the workshop, participants will explore how key SAP S/4HANA modules work together within the Global Bike 4.3 environment, focusing on real-world business processes and end-to-end integration.

Who is this event for?

This event is designed exclusively for professors and are interested in academic and professional development in SAP and enterprise systems.

What will you learn?

Participants will gain a comprehensive understanding of the following SAP S/4HANA modules:

SAP SD – Sales and Distribution

  • Master Data Management: Managing customers, materials, and pricing conditions
  • Pre-Sales and Sales Order Processing: Quotations, sales orders, and availability checks
  • Delivery and Billing Management: Best practices for shipping and invoicing
  • Credit Management and Customer Service: Credit limits and after-sales processes

SAP MM – Materials Management

  • Procurement and Inventory Management: Optimizing purchasing and stock control
  • Material Requirements Planning (MRP): Automating demand forecasting and procurement needs
  • Invoice Verification: Streamlining payment processes

SAP PP – Production Planning

  • Demand Planning and MRP: Forecasting demand and calculating material needs
  • Master Data and Production Orders: Configuring materials, routings, and production orders
  • Execution, Control, and Integration: Managing material flows and production costs

SAP WM – Warehouse Management

  • Inventory and Stock Movement Management: Real-time control of goods movements
  • Storage Location Strategies and Handling Units: Optimizing space and batch control
  • Integration and Cross-Docking: Connecting WM with other SAP modules

Event Details

  • Dates: April 13, 14, 15, and 16
  • Time: 8:00 AM – 12:00 PM (GMT-5)
  • Format: Live online workshop

Each day will focus on one SAP module:

  • April 13 – SAP Sales and Distribution (SD)
  • April 14 – SAP Materials Management (MM)
  • April 15 – SAP Production Planning (PP)
  • April 16 – SAP Warehouse Management (WM)

Benefits of Participating

  • End-to-end understanding of SAP S/4HANA logistics and operational processes
  • Practical insights for teaching and classroom application
  • Live interaction with an experienced SAP instructor
  • Opportunity to ask questions and exchange experiences with other educators

How to Register

Registration is required to participate.
Sign up now to secure your spot and be part of this exclusive learning experience for SAP educators. 

Don’t miss this opportunity to elevate your SAP teaching methods and empower your students with real-world SAP S/4HANA knowledge. We look forward to having you with us!

entry.title: Security Domain is not defaulted as per Logged-in user’s SD during Item / Class creation in SF LMS
entry.content:
Product: SAP SuccessFactors Learning Management System (LMS)

  • Issue Description:
    During learning creation activities in SF LMS (e.g., Item creation or Class creation), the Security Domain is not automatically fetched from the system as per logged-in user’s assigned Security Domain. The user is required to manually select the Security Domain every time.

Current Behavior:

  • Security Domain must be manually selected during learning creation

Expected Behavior:

 Security Domain should be automatically fetched based on:

  • Logged-in user’s assigned Security Domain

Question / Request:

  • Is this a known limitation in SF LMS?
  • Is there any configuration or best practice to default the Security Domain?

entry.title: ‌🇳🇿 ‌Webinar: KiwiSaver
entry.content:
For the first time in many years, KiwiSaver is experiencing legal changes that impact payroll. The most notable change is an increase in the default contribution rates to 3.5% and the introduction of a new Temporary Rate Reduction process. These changes will require updates to your payroll system, and certain customer actions will be necessary to adjust both employee and employer contribution rates.

During this session, we will detail the legal changes and explain the updates released by SAP to accommodate these new requirements. We will also outline the necessary steps to update contribution rates for April 1, 2026.

Key Takeaways:

  • Gain an understanding of the new KiwiSaver requirements.
  • Learn about the SAP updates supporting the new Temporary Rate Reduction and understand how the infotype records should be maintained. 
  • Learn how to utilize an existing SAP program to update KiwiSaver records to the new contribution rates. 
  • Have the opportunity to ask any questions about these changes.

Who Should Attend:
All SAP NZ Payroll customers and partners are welcome to join.  

The session will be recorded and stored in the New Zealand Payroll - Human Capital Management Localization WorkZone group afterwards.

Please register your attendance so that you can be advised if there is any last minute change to scheduling.

REGISTRATION LINK
